title: Cyclopentanone-2-carboxyanilide as a Gravimetric Reagent for Vanadium(IV)
author: SANAT KUMAR MANDAL
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of Chemistry, Vivekananda Mahavidyalaya, Burdwan 713101, West Bengal 

 Department of Chemistry, The University of Burdwan, Burdwan 713101, West Bengal. 

  Manuscript received 4 June 1976, revised 29 November 1975 : accepted 6 December 1975  

 Vanadium(IV) (6-20 mg) has been precipitated from solutions of  p H 5.0-6.0 with cyclopentanone-2-carboxyanilide and determined by weighing the complex, VO(C 12 H 12 O 2 N) 2  after drying at 110°. Ag + , Tl + , Cr 3+ , CrO 4  2-  do not interfere with the determination and the interference due to Cu 2+ , Ni 2+ , Co 2+ , Zn 2+ , Hg 2+ , Fe 3+ , Al 3+ , Ce 3+ , Zr 4+ , WO 4  2-  or MoO 4-  has been prevented by using ammonium acetate or potassium iodide. The relative standard deviation is ± 0.23%.
